rationale: The citing paper uses Chandon and Wansink (2002) in its conceptual framework to support specific mechanism claims about promotions increasing consumption via salience and about advertising-related consumption effects. These claims are then used to motivate directional expectations for category correlates in the net-impact analysis, so the citation does more than provide generic background. The cited paper is not reused methodologically, but it meaningfully informs the conceptual setup of the consumption component in the citing study.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Chandon and Wansink (2002) as conceptual and empirical grounding for the consumption side of promotion effects, especially the role of salience. It uses this grounding to build hypotheses about how category characteristics should relate to net unit impact.
